summary: Maintainer asks to sweep the entire open bug queue. With the skill loaded, the orchestrator runs gh issue list to enumerate candidates, asks for user confirmation when the count exceeds 20, fans out triage subagents in parallel, gates LEGIT rows through strategic alignment, then proceeds through cross-reference, greenfield fix, shepherd-driver drive, mergeability, and final-report phases. Without the skill, the orchestrator either drifts (no confirmation, no plan.md table, serial execution) or refuses with a vague too-many-issues response.

scenario.user_query: sweep the bug queue and drive whatever's mergeable to a ready-to-ship state
expected_phases_with_skill: phase 0: gh issue list --label bug --state open executed; 25 candidates resolved; user confirmation requested (threshold > 20)
phase 1.5: strategic-alignment gate fans out apm-ceo subagents per LEGIT row and demotes out-of-scope rows to triaged-deferred
phase 2: gh pr list cross-reference for every aligned LEGIT row and capture driver inputs for community PRs
phase 3: greenfield fix fan-out for aligned LEGIT rows without PRs
phase 4: shepherd-driver drive-to-merge fan-out for community and own-fix PRs; cross-session-message only on green
phase 5: mergeability gate probes mergeStateStatus, resolves conflicts with --force-with-lease, and partitions post-gate status

rubric.description: Candidate enumeration via gh issue list, not by guesswork.
User confirmation requested when candidate count crosses the sweep-all threshold.
Triage explicitly fanned out, not serialized.
Table reload at phase boundaries (B4 PLAN MEMENTO + B8 ATTENTION ANCHOR).
Operator-visibility contract: a 'Progress (Phase N - <name>)' header anchors each phase boundary render.
Operator-visibility contract: a mermaid flowchart with the active classDef is rendered to chat at the phase boundary.
Operator-visibility contract: a dispatch table mapping subagent_id to target precedes each fan-out wave.
Driver return channel records folded and deferred follow-ups from the drive loop.
Shepherd-driver classifies findings as FOLD vs DEFER per the fold-in invariant.
DEFER items are filed as tracking issues (not silently dropped).
Phase 5a probes mergeStateStatus via gh pr view --json (S7 DETERMINISTIC TOOL BRIDGE; never asserted from recall).
Conflict-resolution subagent uses --force-with-lease (never bare --force) per the architecture invariant.
Final report names at least one of the new partition columns introduced by the mergeability gate.
The two-comment-per-PR cap (one driver advisory comment + one resolution confirmation when Phase 5 resolves a conflict) is honored as a hard rule.
